title: Learning Product Outcomes, Command Vocabulary, and Session Contract
description: A science museum wants a Pokedex kiosk command but has not defined its learner decision, audience needs, command vocabulary, session boundaries, or evidence of understanding. Learners cumulatively produce bounded learner-facing command product charter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define learner population, science or coding decision, command journey, accepted failure, feedback, and observable learning value.
Specify commands, arguments, examples, aliases, case and whitespace policy, invalid forms, and stable results as a small language.
Model ready, prompting, parsing, dispatching, waiting, reporting, aborted, closing, and closed states with legal transitions.
